reviews.review_msg: Lectures are entertaining and easy to follow but she makes lots of mistakes. Course material is pretty straight forward. As long as youre on top of practice questions you'll be fine.

She's very passionate about teaching but her lectures tend to be unorganized and she'll often make errors in her notes. Other than that, she's pretty good at teaching the course. Quizzes were simple and midterms were fair. Final exam was also significantly easier that the previous tests.

Marni is passionate about Linear Algebra, but she isn't the best at teaching. Lectures aren't organized very well, felt a little rushed most of the times. Tests are easy and reasonable. Actually study for your quizzes, understand the concepts, and you will be fine.

Right before the final as I walked by her she looked me in the eyes and then started talking to a TA about how all the calculus students had a dead look in their eyes. Her lectures were pretty good, engaged with the students a lot, and I felt like the tests were fair. Only giving a low rating because i am mad i had to write this final after calc.
Lectures are a little messy at times, highly recommended to do practice outside of class with the material. Quizzes taken directly from homework, very easy exams.
Go to Lecture. Do practice questions. No reason you won't get a better grade than you would with any  other professor teaching Math232 at SFU.
Amazing prof
He's fair, explains concepts very good as well! I enjoyed his classes, midterms and final were at the right level of difficulty.
The GOAT of surrey

He was my favourite prof this semester. As the other reviews say, don't speak in his class. His notes were great, and the midterms/final were very fair in their content. He gave numerous past finals/practice exams, which were super helpful. He was always willing to help after class or at office hours. I implore you to take 232 with him if you can.
Justin is amazing at teaching and his homework/practices correlate well with the exams. He's got strong ears though and he'll call you out for talking or whispering, I think it's valid though

Amazing professor, his lectures slides are very clear and easy to understand. Very fair exams/quizzes. Responds fairly quickly to emails and very accessible during office hours.

Dr Justin Chan is the basically the Surrey Equivalent Dr Jamie Mulholland

Absolute GOAT

Justin is a great man and deserves unlimited huzz. Saved my dog from a tree and funded my moms chemo.

Lecture notes were detailed and his exams were easy, given you took the time to memorize his lecture notes and go through his practice exams a few times.

He'll call you out if you talk while he's teaching, but you're there to learn so it's fair.

Justin is great. Gives you the benefit of the doubt on midterms and finals, teaches exactly what's going to be on his tests, prepares you well, and knows how to teach the content well. I would take any class with Justin again if I could, truly one of the top profs at SFU Surrey.

Good professor, explains concepts very well and marks are generous on assignments and quizzes. Midterm and final are a bit difficult, as long as you keep up with the material should be no problem. Sometimes difficult to get a hold of outside of class, and gives loose answers to questions asked in class. Don't speak while he's speaking.

Amazing professor. Notes are super well curated, and cover the topics amazingly. Cares for his students and super respecting for sicknesses or extenuating circumstances. Test's were extremely fair. Respect him while he is teaching and do not talk or he will call you out.

Dr. Chan was amazing for MATH 232 I literally could not recommend him enough. His exams and quizzes are super fair and no more difficult than the homework he assigns. Make sure to try and do some practice questions after each lecture and don't be afraid to use youtube to supplement the more complex topics as there is lots of great videos out there!

232 is a difficult course, but he did a good job teaching it. Passionate about the materials in lectures, and is available in long office hours to help out, usually at least 3 hours per week. Pacing of the course is very fast, but it's not the instructor's fault.
Excellent clear prof for linear algebra, is definitiely the guy you wanna have. He is approachable for asking questions and his lectures are clearly organized.

Justin Chan is probably the best math proff I've had in a while. He actually cares about his students and is super approachable. Exams were very fair. Make sure to religiously do the textbook questions over and over if you want to succeed in this course. Highly recommend this guy

Seriously, Justin is the best teacher to learn a brutal subject like linear algebra from. Maybe even better than Brenda Davison. He made his own set of lecture notes to simplify them for normal students to grasp. That alone was a lifesaver. Some students will complain about how hard he controls the class (no talking whatsoever) but I loved it.

His notes are very good and will help you alot, he will answer every question you ask, but please do not ask him stupid/obvious questions that waste class time because his section would fall behind the others constantly. He marked the assignments very generously compared to other sections so free grade boost. Exam/quiz questions were based on textb

Justin tackled each of the 10+ questions asked per lecture willingly and elaborately. Even though he spoke a bit quietly at times, his explanations were generally clear, and, honestly, I enjoyed the 'no talking during the lectures' aspect since it allowed me to focus on the content. TAs marked nicely; email responses were quite prompt.

1. Don't talk (or even whisper) when he's speaking - he will call you out
2. 232 exams hardly had any conceptual problems, so make sure to grind the mechanical textbook problems
3. Justin is a nice guy and does not half-ass with marking and replying to emails
4. Marking seemed generous (at least for my section)
5. Dry but helpful lectures

helpful and kind
meh at teaching, but it's linear algebra after all
unintentionally funny at times, which makes lectures more enjoyable
all assessments are fair game
study well and you'll do great!

Dr. Chan was a good professor who was very responsive to questions and open to office hours. Quizzes and Exams are not hard if you prepare for them. He does have a tick with people talking in his lectures and will stop and call people out but its not that big of a deal and makes sense.

TAKE THIS ADVICE SERIOUSLY. Please do not take a class with this woman. Her attitude is brush off and arrogant. She doesn't control her classroom, her notes are a mess and her 50 min tests are 20 questions long. If you don't want to hate math when you finish the semester please listen and avoid at ALL COSTS.
Nice lady, interesting lectures, quick with the regrading process, just as good as Jamie imo.
would not recommend this course to my worst enemy.

I don't like Brenda's teaching if I'm going to be honest. She doesn't know how to even do the questions she goes over in the notes. In addition, she goes over everything so quickly. If I were you I would strongly recommend NOT taking Brenda Davison's course!

Brenda is a super nice lady and really explains most concepts well. only issues was that she makes quite a lot of mistakes during the lectures which she mostly corrects but its not that big of an issue. Midterms were overall pretty fair just due the practice material she provides and youre good

MATH232 with Professor Davison can be challenging, but her lectures and office hours make it manageable. Definitely use the textbook and do all the homework - it really helps. Unstuck AI was a lifesaver for exam prep too.

Favorite math prof I've had at SFU. Explains things clearly, gets straight to the point, always available to answer questions and truly seems like she cares about you succeeding and understanding. She has a passion for this and it shows, it passed onto me. No one said it's an easy course if you don't put in the work, btw. Thank you so much Brenda.

Pace is really fast and it is very important to grasp the first few week's material since its all built on that. Brenda's pretty good and she has past lecture recordings on youtube. Midterms and final are easier than anticipated.
Good prof, just do the work and you'll get a good grade, this is not a hard class, don't listen to anyone who tells you it is.

She is funny and knowledgeable but linear algebra is just a difficult topic to grasp. She sometimes goes too fast and her work for some examples is hard to follow. Personally, near the end of the course I just stopped trying to understand her lectures and learned from the textbook instead.

She's alright, not the best prof I have had. I felt like she went too fast for some of the materials. However, she is definitely better than few other profs at the math department. You definitely need to put in your own work and practice as much as you can.

Linear algebra was the hardest class the I have ever done. However, Brenda's office hours made Math 232 less stressful. She's a very forgiving person, and does everything she can to pass every. If you want to do well in the course, you'll have to study a lot.

Math 232 is a hard class, not because of the content itself but the content is new and never before seen. However, Brenda makes it bearable through her organization and dedication. She is on time, exams are for the most part, fair and you can tell she wants you to understand the material. This class will require much of your time and dedication.

Dr. Davidson is an excellent professor, if there was a higher rating than 5 she would receive it. She is extremely knowledgeable and passionate. She explains concepts in an engaging way. Her office hours are helpful and you can tell she cares about students. She never hesitates to answer questions either. Take MATH 232 with Dr. Davidson!!

Brenda Davison is a great instructor. If you have the option to take her - do it. 
MATH232 itself is a difficult class, but she explains concepts well and doesn't waste students' time.
Quizzes and assignments are easy and fairly graded. Tests are hard, but still graded fairly.
Do ALL the homework, if possible. It really helps.

Material builds on top of each other and is understandable once enough repetition is done. Professor provided good lectures and slides. Homework is good to do as it allows you to get more practice in and to understand concepts. Tests were also fair. Would take her again for future courses.

Brenda was awesome, but MATH 232 was hard, always be on top of your stuff and pratice ALOT or else its gg for you my friend. Take advantage of her office hours and AW workshops, she and her TA's are veryy knowledgable people. Overall great prof :)

As you probably heard, MATH 232 is not easy, but Brenda was an excellent professor and made the course somewhat enjoyable. My only complaint would be that the biweekly MATLAB assignments felt a little detached from the course as they did not always have to do with exam material. If you want to do well in this course do lots of textbook questions.

Brenda's very passionate about teaching. Midterms were decently hard, but the final was relatively easier. There's lots of homework assigned (not for marks) but practice is definitely needed to do well in the course.

Brenda is absolutely amazing and super knowledgable. She makes concepts crystal clear and always gives extremely detailed, straightforward explanations. There's lots of homework and it moves fast but Brenda is concise and will teach you all the relevant stuff. I also appreciate her humour. MATLAB is easy to learn and exams were tough but fair.

Keshav is a super friendly and really funny. His midterms were really fair on the whole. Make sure you study after the first midterm because the material does get harder, though do-able. Overall I'd say Keshav is a good prof, just make sure you keep up. Final is harder than the midterm but mainly just because the material is cumulative.

Nancy is an exceptional professor. She marks very fairly, but is still always willing to listen to your grading appeals. Her lecture notes and clear and concise which is also a huge plus. You need to put in the effort to do well in linear algebra and Nancy is always there to help. She is clearly interested in math and is an inspiring teacher.
Easy midterms, and easy finals. Midterm 2 was easier than midterm 1
Great prof. Her exams are fair and shes good at explaining concepts. Really enjoyed her lectures, and will look for other classes to take with her.
Very fair midterms , and good at explaining concepts
Better than many of other Maths professors.
He is very approachable and teaches pretty well

Jas is an exceptional professor. His teaching style is straightforward and he explains concepts clearly. The quizzes, midterms, and finals were all based on the participation homework he gave us. If you take the time to do the work you will do good. He also makes straightforward and to-the-point reminders and announcements.

Jas is a really good prof and his lectures are very clear and understandable. He is also very helpful during the office hours. This course is not supposed to be easy but he explains the concepts very clearly and makes them less complicated. All course content was well reflected in quizzes, assignments, and midterms. Highly recommend!

Jas is great with math and is good at lecturing. He is approachable and encourages questions. However, he teaches only using mathematical language similar to the textbook, and often rushes over new concepts to end classes early, which made new concepts hard to grasp often. Homework is minimal and is mostly to prep you for tests.

Jas is really nice and patient! Helps a lot if you go to office hours. I got a meh mark bc the course content is really hard and I didn't study as much as I should've for the final lol so it's a skill issue. However Jas himself is really good. Just be sure to do all the hw, ask for help EARLY if you don't get stuff. Tests cover hw material to the T

This man is bad at every thing he does. He was bad enough as a prof. He was chronically late and felt unenthusiastic and uninvolved. He's doing worse as the math coordinator, as I heard that he made most if not all lower division math course midterms on the same day and didn't change anything despite the numerous complaints and requests.

His lectures might be boring to some of the students. Because his voice is not high nor enthusiasm. But he will write his notes while he is speaking. And that is the most important key point for this corse!!!! It helps you know what is the steps when he solving the problems, what is he talking and how is he thinking, and how you should learn MATH.

He is an excellent teacher: clear, organized, informative. And there is something charming in his attitude: he is not really snob, but he knows to keep a distance - he is hot and cold at the same time. He makes me feel like it was in my childhood, when I tried to be a good boy. I know that other students feel similar. Nice experience.
Very nice prof. Definitely he loves teaching. Classes are not tedious. In fact, I spend quite lot of time on assignments. Fair marking. Midterms are passable.
Likerecommend this prof. midterms follow both assignment questions and class notes. marking is very fair. classes are well structured. he gives clear examples to the course is not stuffy.

I started skipping lectures about halfway into the semester, his Italian accent was just too strong for me to adapt to, and my listening comprehension did not improve over the semester. Just do the textbook questions and read the book yourself, you really won't be missing out on anything.
do all the homework and you'll get A

I love Simone. He is the all the possible qualities you could want from a professor. Really encourages visits to his office hours to pinpoint problems for each specific student. Very nice lectures; his english is very good despite having a thicc spaghetti accent. Quizzes are of the same format of the textbook, with some numbers changed up. SIMONEEE

The quizzes are directly from the homework each week usually with the exact same values. So do the homework and understand it and you will ace to quizzes. -10%. Exams are easy as long as you have been keeping up with the homework and done the extra questions related to each assignment. Know your definitions for true/false. Ask questions in class.

People who complain about his quiz have no excuse cause his quiz questions are in the homework so just do the homework. Tests are really fair too, nothing i havent seen before in the homework/ practice exams/ quizzes. Overall a solid prof. Only critique is that his writing is horrible. Gives you all the tool to succeed unlike other profs.
Take him! He's knowledgeable, you won't be stressed with him at all, but the course is heavy!
Asik sih, asik sendiri
Best prof! Love him!

Professor Navid is the best instructor at FIC. He is really passionate with what he teaches and explains everything in detail. You need to read the textbook to fully grasp all of the information as linear algebra has a lot of content.
best math teacher at fic, get ready to self learning follow brenda davison on youtube

Navid is a very great teacher as he always gives you the support you needed during and after class. He is very understanding for his students and explains everything thoroughly in class, that you don't need any self-learning outside of classes. The course is tough but he managed to organise it neatly and this really helps.

Self learning is pretty important in this course but I' not sure why it's so different from the lecture video of SFU but overall doing some prelecture helps. The other thing I'm not sure is why his answers to the hw is incomplete. He is a good teacher the way he's teaching inspired everyone from different angles, take his course!

Only take any course with her if you're comfortable teaching yourself the material in it's entirety. Lectures were pointless, would say stay away completely if she ever starts using her own tests instead of department supplied ones.

Pretty solid prof, although during her course the quizzes were due 2 days before the homework for that section, so be prepared to study ahead if you're lazy like me. Midterms were crazy accessible for a 200 math class and as long as you read the instructions and do the practice you'll squeeze by no problem

Lecture is interactive-ish. Her exams are always so specific, they cover multiple tiny section instead of whole largely covered and focused on units. Do not ever go based off the reviews she gives because the exams always have next to nothing she tells you to cover. For the final hope you know the random 5 of the 30 sections very well. Weekly quiz

Seda was a nice prof and her midterms were very fair. They were very similar to the reviews we would do in class. Her lectures are alright they did get a bit boring sometimes tho. overall W prof would take again.
Lectures are very confusing. Better off teaching yourself.
Mr randall Mr randall, oh how I love you. I started this course as a man, and ended as a pregnant woman. Amazing lectures so goated. Bring your meds
someone get randall his meds

Avoid. You'll need to watch video lectures before each class and the actual classes are him half-doing questions (his favorite line: "I'll let you do that on your own"). Tests also suck. Way harder than the mock ones, and will always have many questions on something you've never seen before. Midterm 1 had multiple questions with median scores of 0.
his teaching skills are 2.5/5 barely passable.

Pt 1. His in class lectures of him doing practice problems consist of him saying, "this part gets quite involved so Ill skip to the next part," then skipping ten steps to the answer. He mumbles incoherently and goes off on random tangents mid explanation. He copies his premade notes with bad organization which makes it difficult to follow.

Pt 2. When he strays from copying his notes, he makes mistakes that he either cant figure out how to correct or corrects things without explaining how. He makes the class watch hours of pre class lectures with half the content being irrelevant to the notes because he digresses.

Pt 3. Brenda from Burnaby has lectures half the length of his in way better detail.Midterms are impossible to pass.He blatantly said at the beginning of the semester that we will never have enough time to complete midterms and quizzes in this class (the midterms and quizzes make up 42% of the grade scheme)

Pt 4. I didnt believe it until I opened a 60 point 50 minute midterm. The average was 47 after a 3 point curve. The second average was hidden. He once got mad that people only showed up to lectures in the last 10 minutes on quiz days and threatened to shut the doors to cut people off. He "accidentally" ran out of quiz sheets once.

He crossed a section off in the uploaded notes and explicity said in the video that it was to be skipped I show up to the quiz today and its one of the two questions on the quiz People asked him about it and he said he chose to teach it this year but only in person Now theres no notes to study for this section Pyke gets off on failing his students
he ruiend my life
If you want to pass MATH 232, do not take his class. Trust me, just trust me.
give her the meds.
Did I forgor my meds again?????
This prof loved me alot.
Absolutely goated. The best prof u will ever have. 100% guarantee u will get A+ just like I did. Scales really well. Went from 2% to 3%. ALL HAIL KING PYKE.

randall is by far the worst prof i have ever and probably will ever take. expects hours among hours of pre-lecture videos that must be watched. he gives lots of practise but the tests are never anything like them. i BARELY passed that course after making it my entire life for 3 months. bless the textbook because its the only teacher in that class

He's not that bad. The class is very fast-paced, and he makes it clear at the start that you need to make time to study linear algebra, whether you like it or not. He offers good review packages before each exam and the questions are similar to those ones. The computing assignments were the most difficult portion, even with some coding knowledge.
Almost failed but he helped me a lot
Very easy professor that can teach very well! Also very kind when you ask questions. Very very good!!

He's kind but can't teach. You'll need to teach yourself (I used the textbook). Attend lectures because he'll occasionally drop a formula not in the book. Do all assignments in full and train for speed for exams and quizzes. Quizzes are paced extremely fast, exams a bit slower but the content is harder. A deep understanding is required for success.
countless hours of videos that he expects you to watch prior to lectures, lectures are terrible too. hes just bad bro. honestly just read the textbook instead. will not curve either
Very clear and easy to understand lectures. Was a pleasure to take this course!
Professor was very great, final was very short and very easy. People that can't do math can't succeed in this course
"Sanity is insanity and insanity is sanity here. Welcome to the underworld." - A victim of Randall Pyke.

Dont take - Run away - very bad -cant teach-cant mark- final exam was worth 127 marks across 28 pages. Avoid at all costs, take in some other term, take in another campus, take in another school, take in another country, but NEVER take with randall.
very great!

Whatever you do, DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM!! Defer it till the summer, take it in Burnaby, take it at another university for all that matters because no matter how hard you try, you can never know what to expect. His courses WILL tank your gpa no matter how hard you try. For your own sanity, don't make yourself suffer.

unless you like theory, you'll HATE it here. You will not get by by just doing textbook questions, practice, or knowing how to compute certain questions. On the midterms, you need to know WHY everything is the way it is. It's a headache, bc 1) he goes off on unrelated tangents and 2) when he finally gets to the point, you're just.. more lost. sos.

Doesn't know how to teach, very difficult grader. Do not take this class with him if you wanna pass. For all students including SEE and MSE students, its better to just miss however many terms necessary if you cant find a class without Pyke than to take it with him. Go to Burnaby, wait more terms, DO NOT take this class with him.

Avoid this man at all costs!!! His quizzes are extremely difficult (1 quiz had a 15% average), and his exams are nothing like the practice exams he gives you. He is unfair and incredibly misleading. Computing assignments are difficult and time consuming. His video and in person lectures are useless. Be ready to learn the entire course on your own…

Randall is a great prof.

You have find time to watch videos lectures before the class.

Randall Pyke is by far the WORST professor you can ever get. If you get his class drop it IMMEDIATELY! Not worth risking your sanity trying to decode his pre-lecture videos. He writes with his left hand, covering what he just wrote, so get ready to write fast. Honestly don't even bother going to the lectures unless you want to lose brain cells.

This class isn't easy and Pyke isn't a great lecturer but these ratings are completely unhinged. The quizzes are pretty similar to the practice problems he gives. If you aren't capable of doing the practice problems then obviously you won't do well. There are many problems he gives, but a lot of them tend to be simple computations

He doesn't know how to teach, nothing makes sense in his class ranging from his lectures to his assignment description. HIS QUIZZES ARE NOTHING LIKE THE HOMEWORK OR THE LECTURES. I have never met an instructor this bad
Good lectures. Gives enough time for quizzes. Very accessible.
Horrible lectures, doesn't know what he's talking about. Online videos are even worse. Can't understand a thing

Mayor of Yappington, Duke of Yappinborough, The Yap Knight Returns, Rise of the Yapper, 21 Yappin.
If you like yapping and brain-rot lectures he's your man.
Horrible explanations and convoluted manner of teaching. Would not recommend

It is naive to believe you will pass. 

I do not care what marks you previously got. I do not care what your GPA currently is. All that is about to change when you first set foot into randall pyke's classroom. There is no method to the madness, and logic is indeed scarce. 

Sanity is insanity and insanity is sanity here.
Welcome to the underworld.

The beginning of the course is the hardest part with tons of concept. Sometimes the textbook and assigment are more helpful than lecture, so do the assigment. His midterm and final are easy, if u fully understand the assignment.

His midterms and final were super easy..actually I have never seen that such a kind of easy final exam...so you can easily get good grade..but I think he was not helpful.When I went to the workshop, he always held small lecture to the specific people..So I always use TA..but overall he is good prof.
helpful good instructor!
A good Prof. Comes to the workshop and helps students understand problems. Starts off with basics to make sure that students digest a bigger problem properly.

You know he is uhhh you know kind of not a very good uhhh prof you know? You will come to hate that phrase in his class! His lectures are terrible barely explains anything and his tests were completely different than the assignments and the textbook which you will have to read to clarify his lecture notes. Would not recommend uhh you know?
